_comment: Every path in a public skill repo belongs to exactly one class (Skill Repo Spec v1 s9).
TOOL    = code, SKILL.md, docs. Public, hand-written, contains no data.
FIXTURE = tests and examples. Public, but SYNTHETIC and produced by tools/make_fixtures.py.
DATA    = anything a real run produced. NEVER git-tracked; lives in the private companion
config, resolved at runtime by tools/datadir.py. The repo ships only the schema.
Enforced by tools/data_boundary.py, in .githooks and in CI.
metrics/live-runs.jsonl is DATA and always was: Step 7 tells a real run to append one line per
source touched, so the file accumulated which products the operator priced, which retailers they
bought from, and which region they ship to. pii_guard passed it clean every single time -- there
is no email or phone in a domain slug. The tool knowledge those runs produced (which source works
for which channel, which retailers block a scrape) is generalizable and belongs to the public
skill: it was distilled by hand into reference/source-reliability.md. The observations themselves
did not, and now resolve from outside the repo.
metrics/gh-api-cache.json is DATA too -- a real run's cached GitHub API reads. It was already
gitignored before this manifest existed, so declaring it changes nothing on disk; it makes the
class explicit, and it earns its `.example` schema like any other DATA path. Its contents are
public GitHub facts rather than the operator's life, so it is a far milder case than live-runs --
but 'this particular cache happens to be harmless' is a judgement call, and the boundary is worth
more than the exception. It is rebuildable: delete it and re-run the gate authenticated.
reference/scenario-eval/scenarios.jsonl is a FIXTURE, generated by tools/make_fixtures.py.
An earlier draft of this manifest argued it did NOT need a generator, on the grounds that it is
hand-authored and already clean -- a generator would just re-emit its own contents, proving
nothing. That reasoning was wrong, and it was wrong in the way that matters: it asked whether the
file IS clean, when the generator rule exists to ask whether it can STAY clean. Its rows are
buy_intent strings -- realistic shopping requests naming a real product shipped to a real place.
The next agent told to add a scenario needs exactly such an example and is already holding one:
the operator's actual orders. That row would look completely at home here, would pass review
because it is indistinguishable from its neighbours, and pii_guard would miss it -- a product name
is not a PII pattern. The generator makes the move fail instead of pass: a real purchase cannot be
regenerated, so a pasted one no longer matches its generator and data_boundary BLOCKS at commit.
The contents are unchanged; only the source of truth moved into the generator's case table.
